text.title: web-scraping-resilience Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for web-scraping-resilience as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using a fixed delay (e.g., always 1000ms) between requests.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fixed intervals create a detectable timing pattern. Bot-detection systems flag predictable inter-request intervals as automation signatures.

failure-mode.description: Retrying immediately in a tight loop (no delay between attempts) when a request fails.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rapid retries hammer the server, accelerate ban risk, and do not give transient errors (DNS glitch, overload) time to resolve.

failure-mode.description: Relying on a single fixed User-Agent string across all sessions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rotating UA per session is trivial; a fixed UA is a persistent fingerprint that bot-detection systems log and block after a threshold of requests.
